codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 5 blockers and 7 majors (one marked OBSERVATION_ONLY). Blockers: (1) --pre-head argument referenced in M7/op-order but absent from M2, Script Interface, and actual implementation; (2) AC3 inconsistency between ticket text and JSON wording + non-isolated test; (3) M4 vs M7 conflict on where head_sha binding occurs; (4) S1 says exit 1 but exit table says exit 2 for push.sh missing; (5) AC1/5b/7 write mock push.sh to ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh which is the live repo (would overwrite real hook). Majors include AC9 vacuous pass, AC10 path form not enforced, AC2/4/6 can be masked by missing push.sh, AC2/3/6 missing grant-preservation assertions, AC7 stderr capture ambiguity. One observation: AC5b broken-shebang approach is sound on Linux.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: 5 Codex blockers accepted and surfaced as blocking QA issues. 4 Codex majors accepted. 2 Codex findings reclassified: os.execv-vs-subprocess proof (Codex #11) → OBSERVATION_ONLY (AC1/AC7 capture mechanism provides indirect evidence; R6 specifies the requirement); AC10 grep brittleness (Codex #9) → merged into M13/path-form finding (QA-M7-A).

iteration_2_fixes_assessed.QA-1_AC5_split: CONFIRMED ADDRESSED — AC5 is now AC5a (push.sh absent → exit 2, grant preserved) and AC5b (execv OSError → sentinel unlinked, exit 2). Split is structurally correct.
iteration_2_fixes_assessed.QA-2_grant_head_sha_mismatch_AC: CONFIRMED ADDRESSED — AC6 correctly isolates step-9 binding check (--pre-head passes at step 5, grant.head_sha differs). However, the underlying --pre-head interface inconsistency (see NEW-B1) means neither AC3 nor AC6 can be correctly tested without resolving B1 first.
iteration_2_fixes_assessed.QA-3_verdict_warn_AC: CONFIRMED ADDRESSED — AC7 covers verdict=warn path. However, AC7 has two remaining issues: (1) mock push.sh overwrites live hook (see NEW-B5); (2) stderr capture wording is ambiguous (see NEW-M4).
iteration_2_fixes_assessed.QA-4_SESSION_ID_unset_AC: CONFIRMED ADDRESSED — AC8 added with exit 2, correct env -u invocation, CLAUDE_SESSION_ID in stderr check.
iteration_2_fixes_assessed.QA-5_push_md_update_ACs: PARTIALLY ADDRESSED — AC9 and AC10 added. AC9 is vacuous (grep pattern never existed in push.md). AC10 does not enforce the tilde-form path requirement from M13. Both are blocking.

issues.title: --pre-head argument referenced in M7 and operation order but absent from M2, Script Interface, commands/push.md, and actual implementation
issues.location: ticket-20260524-125300-push.md: M2, M7, operation-order step 5, Script Interface Invocation block; acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-push.json: AC3, AC6; commands/push.md:183; scripts/execute-push.py
issues.finding: M7 states: 'current HEAD must equal --pre-head argument (early check at step 5, before grant read)'. The operation order step 5 states: 'validate equals supplied --pre-head argument'. AC3 and AC6 both describe behavior conditional on '--pre-head' being passed. However: (a) M2 lists only --repo-hash, --remote, --request-id, and --auto — no --pre-head; (b) the Script Interface Invocation block omits --pre-head; (c) commands/push.md Step 5 invocation line 183 has no --pre-head; (d) the actual scripts/execute-push.py has no --pre-head argument and no early drift check at step 5. The implementation performs only ONE head drift check (grant.head_sha vs current HEAD), not two. The 'two distinct points' design in M7 cannot be tested or implemented without resolving this contradiction in the spec.
issues.evidence: grep of M2: '--repo-hash <str>, --remote <str>, --request-id <str>, and optional --auto flag' (no --pre-head). grep -c 'pre.head|pre_head' scripts/execute-push.py returns 0. Operation order step 5 says 'supplied --pre-head argument'. commands/push.md line 183: 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py --request-id ... --repo-hash ... --remote ...' (no --pre-head).
issues.recommendation: BA must either: (A) add --pre-head <PRE_HEAD> to M2, the Script Interface Invocation block, the commands/push.md Step 5 example, and all AC invocations (the spec's two-drift-check design requires it); OR (B) rewrite M7 to specify a single drift check (grant.head_sha vs current HEAD only, removing the --pre-head argument concept entirely) and rewrite AC3 to match. Choose one coherent design. The current spec has a required CLI argument that does not exist anywhere in the interface definition.

issues.title: AC3 wording inconsistent between ticket.md and acceptance-criteria JSON; test does not cleanly isolate step-5 drift check
issues.finding: The ticket AC3 heading reads 'grant.head_sha mismatch (grant written for wrong HEAD)' — which is a step-9 binding check. But the JSON AC3.given describes '--pre-head argument does not match current git HEAD' — which is a step-5 early drift check. These are two different scenarios testing two different steps. The two spec documents are inconsistent on what AC3 tests. Additionally, the AC3 GIVEN sets grant.head_sha to the same stale SHA as --pre-head, so both step 5 and step 9 would fail — the test cannot prove that step 5 is what fires (an implementation that checks only step 9 would also pass AC3, which already has AC6 for that).
issues.evidence: ticket.md AC3 heading: 'grant.head_sha mismatch (grant written for wrong HEAD)'. acceptance-criteria JSON AC3.given: 'The --pre-head argument does not match current git HEAD (step 5 early drift check); the grant head_sha is set to the same stale SHA as --pre-head so step 9 would also fail but step 5 fires first'.
issues.recommendation: Align ticket and JSON wording. If --pre-head is added (per NEW-B1 option A), rewrite AC3 with: current HEAD = current SHA; grant.head_sha = current SHA (valid, step 9 would pass); --pre-head = stale SHA (step 5 fails). This cleanly isolates step 5. Also rename the ticket AC3 heading to match.

issues.title: M4 lists head_sha==current git HEAD as a grant validation field, conflicting with M7 which reserves head_sha as the step-9 binding check
issues.finding: M4 says the script validates the grant with 'head_sha==current git HEAD' as part of grant field checking. M7 says 'grant.head_sha must equal current HEAD (binding check at step 9, after grant validation)'. If M4 already checks head_sha==current HEAD during grant validation (step 8), then M7's 'step 9 binding check' is redundant — and the operation order is contradictory (step 8 checks head_sha, but step 9 also checks head_sha). This ambiguity prevents dev from correctly implementing the two-check design. The M4 list should cover schema/type validation only (verdict in enum, risks is list, expires_at parseable and future, field types correct), while the binding checks (nonce, branch, head_sha, remote, session_id) belong to step 9.
issues.evidence: M4 verbatim: '...checking: file existence, valid JSON, object type, nonce==request-id, branch==current git branch, head_sha==current git HEAD, remote_name==REMOTE, session_id==CLAUDE_SESSION_ID, verdict in {approved, warn, blocked}, risks is array, expires_at is future ISO-8601'. M7 verbatim: '(b) grant.head_sha must equal current HEAD (binding check at step 9, after grant validation)'.
issues.recommendation: Split M4 into two parts: M4 for schema validation (verdict in enum, risks is list, expires_at parseable, all fields present and correct type), and M9-binding (nonce, branch, head_sha, remote_name, session_id equality checks at step 9). This matches the operation order steps 8 and 9.

issues.title: S1 specifies exit 1 for push.sh absent/not-executable, contradicting the exit code table and AC5a which specify exit 2
issues.finding: S1 (Should Have) reads: 'abort with exit 1 and clear error message (push.sh missing/not executable)'. The exit code table on the same page reads: 'exit 2: Setup/infrastructure error: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset, bad CLI args, push.sh missing/not executable, os.execv OSError after sentinel write'. AC5a specifies exit 2 for push.sh absent. The actual implementation (execute-push.py line 258) returns 2. S1 says exit 1 which directly contradicts the exit table, AC5a, and the implementation. This would cause a dev implementing S1 literally to write incorrect exit code logic.
issues.evidence: S1 verbatim: 'if not, abort with exit 1 and clear error message'. Exit table: 'exit 2: push.sh missing/not executable'. AC5a check: 'exit_code: 2'. scripts/execute-push.py: 'return 2' at the push.sh existence check.
issues.recommendation: Change S1 to read: 'if not, abort with exit 2 (infrastructure failure per exit code taxonomy) and clear error message'.

issues.title: AC1, AC5b, and AC7 write mock push.sh to ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh, which maps to the live repo's hooks/push.sh in this environment
issues.finding: ~/.claude is a symlink to /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude (the live repo). AC1 GIVEN states: 'test harness writes a mock push.sh at ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh (chmod 0755)'. AC5b GIVEN states: '~/.claude/hooks/push.sh exists and is chmod 0755... but its shebang points to a path that does not exist'. AC7 references the same protocol as AC1. Running these ACs as specified WOULD OVERWRITE the real hooks/push.sh in the live repo with a mock script, destroying the production push gate. This is a critical safety defect in the test specification.
issues.evidence: ls -la /root/.claude returns: /root/.claude -> /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude. AC1.given contains: 'test harness writes a mock push.sh at ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh (chmod 0755)'.
issues.recommendation: All ACs that require a mock push.sh must redirect HOME to a temporary directory: set HOME=<tmpdir>; create <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh as the mock; invoke execute-push.py by absolute path so HOME redirection only affects push.sh lookup. Add HOME=<tmpdir> to AC1, AC5b, and AC7 invocations. The tmpdir must be cleaned up in the test harness teardown.

issues.title: AC2, AC4, and AC6 can yield false pass (exit 2 instead of expected exit 1) because they do not provision a mock push.sh
issues.finding: The operation order checks push.sh existence and executability at step 6 (BEFORE grant read at step 7, and before binding checks at step 9). AC2, AC4, and AC6 set up grant conditions to test grant validation failures, but none of them ensure push.sh is present and executable. If push.sh is absent (e.g., in a clean test environment), the script will abort at step 6 with exit 2 before it reaches the grant validation or binding check the AC is testing. The test would record exit code 2 instead of 1 — or pass vacuously if the check is exit_code==1. In a CI environment without the live push.sh, all three ACs could give false results.
issues.evidence: execute-push.py lines 252-259: step 5 checks push.sh before reading the grant. AC2 check only asserts exit_code=1, sentinel_absent, stderr_contains_field_name — no push.sh precondition. Same for AC4 and AC6.
issues.recommendation: Add a GIVEN clause to AC2, AC4, and AC6: 'AND a mock push.sh (chmod 0755, any valid shebang) exists at the path resolve via HOME=<tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh, so the script reaches grant validation before aborting'.

issues.title: M13 requires tilde-form path but commands/push.md uses /root/.claude/ and AC10 does not detect this violation
issues.finding: M13 states: 'The invocation in commands/push.md uses the ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py path form'. The Technical Hints section also states: 'ALWAYS python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py — never a relative path, never python3 scripts/execute-push.py'. However, the current commands/push.md line 183 reads: 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py'. The AC10 verification only greps for 'execute-push.py' and checks for argument presence — it does NOT assert that the path uses the tilde form. AC10 would PASS even with the /root/.claude/ absolute path that violates M13. This is a verified path-form violation that the AC does not catch.
issues.evidence: grep -n 'execute-push.py' commands/push.md returns line 183: 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py'. M13 verbatim: 'uses the ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py path form'. AC10.check only verifies 'invocation_line_present', 'invocation_contains_request_id', 'invocation_contains_repo_hash', 'invocation_contains_remote'.
issues.recommendation: Update AC10 to add a check: grep output must contain the literal string 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' (tilde form, not /root/ absolute). Also update commands/push.md line 183 to use ~/.claude form.

issues.title: AC9 is vacuous: the grep pattern 'rm -f.*push-analyst' never existed in commands/push.md, so the AC passes regardless of dev action
issues.finding: AC9 verifier: 'grep -E "rm -f.*push-analyst" commands/push.md exits with code 1 (no match)'. This grep currently exits 1 because no such pattern ever existed in commands/push.md. It will continue to exit 1 after the dev cycle regardless of whether dev updated Step 4 correctly. The negative check is vacuously true. The only useful part of AC9 is the second assertion (grep 'execute-push.py' confirms script is referenced), but that does not verify Step 4 was updated with the correct language.
issues.evidence: grep -E 'rm -f.*push-analyst' /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/commands/push.md exits with code 1 on the CURRENT file before any dev cycle runs. This means the pattern never existed and the AC proves nothing about whether Step 4 was correctly updated.
issues.recommendation: Replace AC9's negative check with positive assertions about Step 4 content: (1) grep for 'orchestrator must NOT' or 'MUST NOT' or 'sole' in the vicinity of Step 4; (2) grep for 'execute-push.py is the sole grant validator'; (3) verify the Step 4 section no longer contains any unlink/rm/os.unlink instruction.

issues.title: AC7 stderr capture mechanism is ambiguous and incorrectly described
issues.location: ticket-20260524-125300-push.md: AC7 THEN clause; acceptance-criteria: AC7.then
issues.finding: AC7 THEN: 'stderr output (captured before execv using subprocess or by reading from the process's stderr fd before exec) contains the warn risks[] contents'. This description is incorrect: 'reading from the process's stderr fd before exec' is not a valid subprocess capture mechanism. After os.execv, the file descriptor is inherited by push.sh — Python cannot read from it. The correct mechanism is to invoke execute-push.py via subprocess.run with stderr=PIPE; the mock push.sh should not write to stderr; after the process completes, assert the captured stderr bytes contain each warn risk string. The AC's description of the capture mechanism is confusing enough that a dev could write a test that does not actually capture stderr correctly, leading to a false pass.
issues.evidence: AC7.then verbatim: 'stderr output captured before execv using subprocess or by reading from the process's stderr fd before exec'. The 'reading from the process's stderr fd before exec' phrase describes an impossible operation from a subprocess perspective.
issues.recommendation: Rewrite AC7 THEN to: 'When invoked via subprocess.run(stderr=subprocess.PIPE), the process stderr bytes contain each element of risks[] from the grant; the EXECUTE_PUSH_TEST_CAPTURE file exists; sentinel exists with result=PASS; grant consumed. Mock push.sh must not write to stderr so captured stderr reflects only execute-push.py output.'

issues.title: AC2, AC3, and AC6 do not assert grant file is preserved (not consumed) on validation/binding failure
issues.finding: The spec states that the grant must not be consumed when validation fails (M8: 'ONLY for approved/warn verdicts, after verdict check passes'). However, AC2 (expired/mismatched grant), AC3 (pre-head mismatch), and AC6 (head_sha binding mismatch) none of them assert 'grant file still exists at grant path'. An implementation that unconditionally unlinks the grant on any failure would pass all three ACs. Only AC4 (blocked verdict) correctly adds 'grant file still exists'. AC2/AC3/AC6 need the same assertion.
issues.evidence: AC4.check includes 'grant_still_exists: true'. AC2.check: {'exit_code': 1, 'sentinel_absent': true, 'stderr_contains_field_name': true} — no grant_still_exists. AC3.check: {'exit_code': 1, 'sentinel_absent': true, 'stderr_indicates_head_mismatch': true} — no grant_still_exists. AC6.check: {'exit_code': 1, 'sentinel_absent': true, 'stderr_indicates_head_sha_mismatch': true} — no grant_still_exists.
issues.recommendation: Add 'grant_still_exists: true' to AC2.check, AC3.check, and AC6.check.

original_blocking_issues_status.QA-1: ADDRESSED — AC5 split into AC5a and AC5b. New issue NEW-B4 (S1 exit code conflict) is related but distinct.
original_blocking_issues_status.QA-2: PARTIALLY ADDRESSED — AC6 added for step-9 binding check. But NEW-B1 (--pre-head absent from interface) and NEW-B2 (AC3 inconsistency) affect the AC3/AC6 pair.
original_blocking_issues_status.QA-3: ADDRESSED structurally. AC7 added for verdict=warn path. Residual issue NEW-M4 (stderr capture ambiguity) is major but not blocking.
original_blocking_issues_status.QA-5: PARTIALLY ADDRESSED — AC9 and AC10 added but both have verifier defects (NEW-M2, NEW-M3).

original_major_issues_status.QA-6: ADDRESSED — all AC invocations now use absolute path /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/execute-push.py. However, NEW-B5 is a new safety concern introduced by the mock push.sh protocol.
original_major_issues_status.QA-7: ADDRESSED — AC1 now has concrete capture protocol specified (EXECUTE_PUSH_TEST_CAPTURE). Residual issue NEW-B5 (mock push.sh overwrites live hook) must be fixed.

qa.overall_assessment: Iteration 2 addressed 4 of the 5 original blocking issues (QA-1 through QA-4) and both major issues (QA-6, QA-7) at the structural level. However, the revision introduced or exposed 5 new blocking issues and 5 new major issues. The most critical are: (1) --pre-head argument is referenced in M7 and operation-order but absent from M2, the Script Interface, and the actual implementation — the two-drift-check design cannot be tested without resolving this; (2) AC1/AC5b/AC7 would overwrite the real hooks/push.sh when run; (3) S1 specifies exit 1 but the exit table, AC5a, and the implementation all say exit 2; (4) M4 and M7 conflict on where head_sha validation occurs. The spec must be revised again before dev work can proceed safely.
qa.approval_notes: REJECTED. 5 new blocking issues (NEW-B1 through NEW-B5) were found that were not present in iteration 1. BA must resolve all 5 blockers and the 5 major issues in iteration 3. The --pre-head interface question (NEW-B1) is the highest priority: the spec's two-drift-check design requires an argument that does not appear in M2, the Script Interface, commands/push.md, or the actual script. BA must decide: add --pre-head to the interface, or remove the two-check concept from M7 and the operation order. The AC1/AC5b/AC7 live-hook overwrite risk (NEW-B5) is a safety-critical defect that must be fixed with HOME isolation in all mock-push.sh ACs.
